name: Mobile phones and market efficiency in developing economies
description: This domain examines how mobile phones affect economic development and market functioning in developing regions, drawing on the well-known case of the fishing industry in Kerala, India, and its subsequent critique emphasizing the importance of complex social systems. It also covers the domestication of mobile payments by underserved South African microentrepreneurs and broader reflections on the unpredictable social and economic impacts of mobile technology.

background_concepts.concept: Law of one price / market price dispersion
background_concepts.why_needed: The fishing-market studies argue mobile phones reduce price dispersion across markets, invoking the economic 'law of one price' as the benchmark for market efficiency without explaining it.

suggested_resources.title: The Law of One Price (Corporate Finance Institute)

name: Community and small-scale cellular/network infrastructure
description: This domain focuses on locally owned and operated telecommunications infrastructure, including small-scale, sustainable cellular networks and community networks financed through community currencies. It also addresses technical challenges such as measurement scheduling strategies in low-resource network environments.

background_concepts.concept: GSM cellular network architecture (base station, spectrum, backhaul)
background_concepts.why_needed: The community cellular papers assume familiarity with how a GSM cell site works (base transceiver station, spectrum licensing, backhaul to the core network) when describing small-scale locally-run networks.

suggested_resources.title: How Cell Phones Work (U.S. FCC consumer guide)
Cellular Concepts and GSM Basics (NPTEL lecture notes)

name: Rural electrification via microgrids and minigrids
description: This domain covers the engineering, planning, and evaluation of microgrid and minigrid systems for rural electrification, spanning cost estimation of micro-hydropower in Nepal, rapid feasibility and business model assessments, DC microgrid power-electronic loss modeling, and multicriteria decision analysis for projects in Sub-Saharan Africa. It also incorporates social perspectives, applying social practice theory to user demand and case studies of standalone, minigrid, and grid-connected system design with irrigation loads in Ethiopia.

background_concepts.why_needed: These engineering papers design standalone minigrids and DC microgrids assuming the reader knows what a microgrid is, its components (generation, storage, loads), and the concept of islanded vs. grid-connected operation.

suggested_resources.title: Microgrids (DOE Tribal Energy Guide)
Microgrid Conceptual Design Guidebook (Sandia National Laboratories)

background_concepts.why_needed: The electrification-project selection paper is built entirely around choosing an MCDA method (weights, criteria, ranking of alternatives) but does not teach what MCDA is or how weighting/scoring works.

suggested_resources.title: An Introductory Guide to Multi-Criteria Decision Analysis (UK Government Analysis Function)
A Practical Guide to Multi-Criteria Decision Analysis (Victoria University of Wellington)

name: Electricity provision and the political economy of the grid
description: This domain explores the political and institutional dimensions of electricity access, including how electoral cycles influence electricity provision and losses in India. It also addresses tools and education for energy access, such as blackout mapping via smartphones (GridWatch), workshops for electricity access educators, and critical debate over whether technology alone can deliver universal energy access.

background_concepts.why_needed: The election-cycle studies claim causal effects of political incentives on electricity supply using quasi-experimental designs (special elections, difference-in-differences) without explaining the identification strategy or parallel-trends assumption.

suggested_resources.title: Difference-in-Differences (World Bank DIME Wiki)
Difference-in-Differences (Causal Inference: The Mixtape)

background_concepts.concept: Distribution losses / aggregate technical & commercial (AT&C) losses
background_concepts.why_needed: The electricity-loss paper measures 'line losses' as a manipulable political variable, assuming the reader understands technical vs. non-technical (theft/billing) distribution losses in a power grid.

suggested_resources.title: Electricity explained: How electricity is delivered (U.S. EIA)

name: Satellite/remote-sensing mapping of infrastructure and socioeconomic conditions
description: This domain applies satellite and remote-sensing imagery with deep learning to map infrastructure and socioeconomic conditions, including poverty mapping, urban village identification, road and infrastructure network extraction, and truck and commercial vehicle traffic detection. It also encompasses nighttime light reconstruction, rare energy infrastructure detection, vulnerability mapping of urban disadvantaged populations, and tracking rural development over decades.

background_concepts.concept: Semantic segmentation and object detection in imagery
background_concepts.why_needed: These papers infer roads, buildings, and infrastructure from imagery using segmentation and detection architectures, assuming the reader knows pixel-level labeling, bounding boxes, and CNN-based vision pipelines.

suggested_resources.title: Beginner's Guide to Semantic Segmentation (V7 Labs)
Semantic Segmentation (MathWorks)

background_concepts.concept: Nighttime lights as a socioeconomic proxy
background_concepts.why_needed: The nighttime-light reconstruction and poverty-mapping papers treat satellite-measured night light as a proxy for economic activity and electrification without explaining the DMSP/VIIRS data or the light-GDP correlation.

suggested_resources.title: Nighttime Lights (NASA Earthdata)

name: AI for power systems modeling and grid resilience
description: This domain uses machine learning and optimization to model, simulate, and strengthen power systems, covering optimal power flow learning, operator learning, power flow benchmark datasets, and open-source microgrid simulation for AI research. It further addresses grid resilience through reinforcement learning and graph-based methods for mitigating cascading failures and fault chains, smart meter data analytics, and integrated resilience platforms like GRIP.

background_concepts.concept: Optimal power flow (OPF) and power flow equations
background_concepts.why_needed: Several papers learn or approximate OPF solutions and use power-flow benchmarks, assuming the reader knows AC/DC power flow equations, generator dispatch, and line-flow constraints.

suggested_resources.title: Introduction to Optimal Power Flow (TU Delft OpenCourseWare)
Lecture Notes on Optimal Power Flow (DTU, Chatzivasileiadis)

background_concepts.why_needed: The fault-chain search and cascading-failure mitigation papers assume understanding of how a single line/component overload propagates into a system-wide blackout.

suggested_resources.title: Tutorial: Cascading Failures (Graph-TIGER documentation)
Grid Systems / Microgrids overview (U.S. DOE Office of Electricity)

name: Solar energy forecasting and rooftop/asset assessment
description: This domain focuses on assessing solar resources and assets, including rooftop solar potential estimation, solar panel mapping via oriented object detection, and experimental study of panel soiling effects in Ghana. It also covers near-term and ultra-short-term photovoltaic power forecasting using satellite and cloud-coverage data, alongside community-oriented solar deployment emphasizing maintenance and empowerment in Haiti.

background_concepts.concept: Photovoltaic energy conversion and system components
background_concepts.why_needed: The solar forecasting and rooftop-potential papers assume familiarity with how PV cells/modules generate power, tilt/orientation, and factors (irradiance, shading, cloud cover) that drive output.

suggested_resources.title: How Does Solar Work? (U.S. DOE)
Photovoltaics and electricity (U.S. EIA Energy Explained)

background_concepts.concept: Oriented object detection in remote sensing
background_concepts.why_needed: The solar-panel mapping paper uses 'oriented' (rotated) bounding boxes rather than standard axis-aligned detection, a specialized variant the reader must understand.

suggested_resources.title: Beginner's Guide to Semantic Segmentation / object detection (V7 Labs)

name: Sensing and data-driven optimization of local energy demand and services
description: This domain leverages sensing and data-driven methods to characterize and optimize local energy demand and services, including granular hourly energy forecasting from monthly invoices, modeling residential and lighting demand in Kenya and East Africa, and causal analysis of cooking fuel access and emissions. It also addresses off-grid system data sharing in Malawi, battery repurposing for solar storage in Pacific Island communities, latrine sensors for sanitation, and community energy usage and storage analysis.

background_concepts.concept: Electricity load profiles and demand modeling
background_concepts.why_needed: These papers estimate hourly load profiles and induced residential demand, assuming the reader understands load curves, peak/base load, and load factor as characterizations of electricity demand.

suggested_resources.title: Electricity explained: Use of electricity / demand (U.S. EIA)

name: Disaster and climate risk detection for infrastructure resilience
description: This domain applies AI and systems approaches to detect and manage disaster and climate risks to infrastructure, including flood-related waste mapping, building damage assessment from satellite imagery, transportation resilience to floods, and landslide and illegal mining detection. It also covers housing stock characterization from drone images for climate resilience, systems architectures for disaster risk reduction, telecommunications-assisted remote monitoring, and shade simulation for heat resilience.

background_concepts.why_needed: The damage-assessment papers classify pre/post-disaster building damage from satellite imagery, assuming knowledge of remote-sensing classification pipelines and change detection.

suggested_resources.title: Beginner's Guide to Semantic Segmentation (V7 Labs)

name: Infrastructure and design in informal settlements
description: This domain examines the physical infrastructure and construction practices of informal settlements, including whether high-mast lighting adequately serves pedestrian paths. It also explores the construction process in its spatial, temporal, and cultural dimensions through the case of Mathare, Nairobi.

name: Rural telecenters and shared-access digital service delivery
description: This domain examines shared-access computing models that bring digital services to underserved rural populations, drawing on systems like KioskNet and assisted distribution platforms such as BlendNet. It also engages with the practical realities of these deployments through case studies of telecenter sustainability failures in the SARI project in India and telecenters fostering inclusion in Bangladesh. Together, the readings weigh both the technical designs and the socioeconomic challenges of making shared digital access viable and inclusive.

background_concepts.why_needed: KioskNet is built on store-and-forward, delay-tolerant networking to serve intermittently-connected rural kiosks, assuming the reader understands DTN and the bundle/mechanical-backhaul model.

suggested_resources.title: Delay- and Disruption-Tolerant Networks: A Tutorial (NASA)

name: Telecom infrastructure and macroeconomic development
description: This domain explores the relationship between telecommunications infrastructure and broader economic growth in developing nations. It focuses on how investment in and access to telecom services contribute to national and regional development outcomes.

background_concepts.why_needed: The macro study estimates telecom penetration's effect on GDP growth using cross-country growth regressions and invokes network externalities/critical mass, terms it does not define.

suggested_resources.title: Network Effects / Externalities (Corporate Finance Institute)

name: Off-grid and mini-grid rural electrification
description: This domain addresses decentralized electrification efforts aimed at bringing reliable power to rural communities lacking grid access. Grounded in a rural microgrid field pilot in India, it considers both the technical goal of ensuring dependable electricity supply and the accompanying social upliftment benefits.

background_concepts.concept: Off-grid solar systems: storage sizing and business models
background_concepts.why_needed: The field-pilot paper discusses battery storage, panel sizing, and pay-as-you-go/tariff business models for off-grid microgrids, assuming the reader understands these energy-access fundamentals.

suggested_resources.title: Microgrid Overview (U.S. DOE Grid Deployment Office fact sheet)
Solar Photovoltaic System Design Basics (U.S. DOE)

name: Power grid reliability monitoring and analytics
description: This domain applies machine learning and natural language processing to understand and improve energy systems, including identifying indicators of energy burden and analyzing power grid outages in Uganda. The readings emphasize data-driven, knowledge-informed policy approaches to grid reliability. A central theme is leveraging analytics to promote an equitable transition of energy systems.

background_concepts.concept: Energy burden and energy equity metrics
background_concepts.why_needed: The energy-burden and equitable-transition papers center on 'energy burden' (share of household income spent on energy) as a policy metric without defining it.

suggested_resources.title: Low-Income Energy Affordability Data (LEAD) Tool & energy burden (U.S. DOE)

name: Rural and last-mile network connectivity measurement
description: This domain focuses on empirically measuring the state of cellular and data connectivity in rural and last-mile areas, particularly through 2G/3G network measurements and assessments of cellular data in India. It connects these measurement efforts to practical applications such as improving emergency preparedness and response in rural areas. The readings collectively characterize connectivity gaps and their real-world implications.

background_concepts.concept: Cellular signal metrics (RSSI, Ec/Io, RSCP, throughput) for 2G/3G
background_concepts.why_needed: These measurement studies report signal strength and quality using metrics like RSSI and Ec/Io and cellular-generation terminology without defining them.

suggested_resources.title: Understanding RSSI, RSRP, and RSRQ (5Gstore)
Beyond Signal Bars: Understanding RSSI, RSRP, SINR & Other Key Metrics (Teltonika)

name: Digital inclusion for marginalized users and small organizations
description: This domain investigates how marginalized users, small non-profits, and underserved communities access and benefit from digital technologies, spanning citizen engagement apps in Windhoek and equitable community internet access during the COVID-19 pandemic. It also addresses securing information systems in an unequal world and understanding the connectivity perspectives of small non-profits serving street children in Bangladesh. The readings emphasize bridging the digital divide across diverse and often overlooked populations.
